title: begonia peltata var. auriformis
intro: Begonia peltata var. auriformis, also known as the 'money plant' or 'gold coin plant', is a rare and unique variety of Begonia with distinctive auriculate-shaped leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Begonia peltata var. auriformis is often used in teas, infusions, or salves, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
